summary: Betty Cantor‑Jackson soundboard delivers an excellent recording of a near‑perfect 1973 three‑set show, highlighted by the China>Rider jam, Half Step and Garcia’s debut “The Cobra” guitar.
review: Fans rave about this complete three‑set performance – set 2 is praised for its extended jams (China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Half Step) and a stellar vocal from Donna Jean, while the third set is called “perfect as hippies ever gonna be.” The Betty Board source provides crystal‑clear sound with only minor audio drop‑outs, and listeners even note the debut of Garcia’s “The Cobra” guitar. Overall the show and its capture are regarded as one of the band’s prime moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently five‑star reviews praise both musical performance and soundboard quality; only one neutral/abstract review does not detract from the overwhelmingly positive consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Cantor‑Jackson board is crisp and well balanced; reviewers mention a few minor AUD drop‑outs but overall clarity and mix are superb.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon: warm‑up first set, near‑perfect second set with extended jams and strong vocal performances, third set described as a hippie‑era high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is highlighted, especially the debut of his “The Cobra” guitar and his ever‑present tasteful tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh receives praise for lush harmonies throughout and notable bass work during Truckin’ > Phil bass > Too.
